questions.prompt: An admin wants to make every new account try Let's Encrypt issuance at creation time. What's the right move?
choices: Manually pass --bootstrap to every future AddDomain invocation
Set a cron job to retry SSL issuance for every account daily
questions.explanation: Setting a Scope is the right surface. Hand-editing config.ini misses the cascade and gets reverted by the Bootstrapper. Per-account --bootstrap works but doesn't generalise. A retry cron is a workaround for a setting that exists.

questions.prompt: Setting a Scope value can trigger service restarts and configuration cascades; a hand-edit to the underlying file does not.
questions.explanation: That's the central reason Scopes exist as a callable interface. A Scope write knows what depends on it and re-renders / restarts accordingly. A hand-edit is silent.
